title: Capstone 04 — Multimodal Document QA (Vision-First PDF, Tables, Charts)

questions.question: Why does the 2026 frontier prefer vision-first late interaction over OCR-then-text on financial PDFs and scientific papers?
options: Vision models are cheaper per page
OCR pipelines mangle rotated tables, dense equations, and chart imagery, losing half the signal
OCR is slower than rendering
OCR cannot run on GPUs

questions.question: What does late interaction mean in ColPali-style retrieval?
options: Each query token scores against every patch token, and per-token maxima are summed via MaxSim
Embeddings are deferred until eval time
Embeddings are computed after the user clicks a result
The reranker only runs on the final candidate

questions.question: Roughly how many patch vectors does a ColQwen embedding produce per page, and what storage problem does that create?
options: Around 2048 patch vectors per page, ballooning raw storage compared with single-vector indexes
Exactly 128 vectors, fitting cleanly in any vector DB
1 vector per page, no storage issue
16 vectors with negligible storage overhead

questions.question: What does DocPruner do in this pipeline?
options: Removes duplicate PDF pages before ingestion
Compresses the multi-vector index by keeping high-signal patches at about 50% with negligible accuracy loss
Rewrites the query embedding for shorter vectors
Crops bounding boxes around evidence regions

questions.question: Why is an OCR text channel still spliced in for some pages?
options: It improves PDF rendering quality
VLMs cannot read images at 180 DPI
OCR is the primary retrieval modality
Equation-dense and table-heavy pages benefit from a text fallback alongside the image

questions.question: Which benchmark does this capstone target for vision-first retrieval evaluation?
options: RewardBench-2
SWE-bench Pro
ViDoRe v3
MMLU-Pro

questions.question: What does the rubric mean by evidence-region grounding?
options: Compression ratio achieved by DocPruner
Re-ranker latency at the p99 tail
Total number of pages retrieved per query
Fraction of cited bounding boxes that actually contain the answer span
